### Session fd6a5e14-6244-48b0-8d56-d16eb5530e6a - 2026-07-19T22:35:57.967Z

- User questioned why files were being deleted from Google Drive during a kit setup process.
- Claude Code explained a sequence issue: 14 files were uploaded before enabling Drive's "convert uploads" setting, so they exist as Office files (.xlsx/.docx) instead of native Google Sheets/Docs formats.
- The problem identified: copy-links (docs.google.com/.../copy URLs) only work for native Google-format files, not Office files; the kit's delivery mechanism relies on these links for buyer access.
- Solution: delete the un-converted copies and re-upload so the conversion setting catches them; the local machine holds masters generated by scripts, so Drive copies are disposable and can be regenerated.
- Alternative approach considered and rejected: uploading without deleting would create duplicate filenames (28 files with 14 identical names), which was deemed a worse outcome.
- Next action: user directed to select and delete all files in the Drive window, then Claude will re-upload the converted batch.
